headline: Dialysis Center Growth Outpaces Prevention Spending 8 to 1
context: Malaysia adds 45 new dialysis centers annually while kidney disease prevention programs receive minimal funding.

cards.big: Malaysia has 1,180 dialysis centers treating 48,000 patients at RM2.5 billion annual cost
cards.sub: Prevention spending on kidney disease is only RM310 million or 12% of treatment cost

cards.big: Malaysia has the 8th highest rate of kidney failure in the world at 280 per million population
cards.sub: Diabetes-driven kidney disease accounts for 65% of new dialysis patients

cards.big: Average dialysis patient costs the health system RM52,000 annually for life
cards.sub: Each prevented kidney failure case saves cumulative millions over patient lifetime

cards.big: Cuba screens 95% of diabetic patients for kidney function through primary care integration
cards.sub: Early detection and management prevents 40% of potential kidney failure cases

cards.big: Building more dialysis centers without preventing kidney disease is a business model not a health strategy
Should kidney disease screening be mandatory for all diabetic patients at every primary care visit
